name: RI success stories
key_idea: Funding enables transformative impact when deployed through trusted intermediaries.
surface_narrative: Grantees praising funder impact — the innovation fund reached €109M and changed organisations.
implicit_narrative: Success is defined by institutional growth and measurable outcomes, not by community-defined change.
metanarrative: The innovation economy frame: social problems are solvable through entrepreneurial funding models. The funder is the hero; the grantee is the grateful recipient.
representative_quotes: Because of the support, recognition and encouragement provided by Rethink Ireland at a crucial early phase, the Hope & Courage Collective has flourished and grown in ways we didn't think possible.
The funding has been transformative for our organisation.

name: Funding system critique
key_idea: Short-term project funding undermines sustainable change.
surface_narrative: Frustration with short-termism, reactive funding cycles, and lack of long-term support.
implicit_narrative: The system is designed for funder accountability, not community resilience. Practitioners are left holding the pieces when projects end.
metanarrative: The critique of neoliberal projectification: complex social problems require sustained commitment, not time-boxed interventions. The measurement culture distorts what counts as valuable work.
representative_quotes: One of the frustrations within mental health services … projects are set up with a start and end date … and then that's sort of it afterwards.
Our goal is to give them the opportunity afterwards to keep doing it … it's about having that kind of lifelong approach to it.

name: Mental health service gaps
key_idea: Systemic failure: services label without supporting, creating institutional harm.
surface_narrative: Mental health services are under-resourced, reactive, and fail to provide continuity of care.
implicit_narrative: The medical model pathologises without healing. A diagnosis becomes a label that follows people rather than a gateway to support.
metanarrative: Institutional betrayal: the systems designed to help instead reproduce exclusion. The gap between policy rhetoric and frontline reality reveals structural indifference.
representative_quotes: Projects are set up with a start and end date … and then that's sort of it afterwards.
The main challenge is sustaining engagement and ownership from players in their mental health journey.

name: Youth MH / school crisis
key_idea: Poverty, anxiety, and school refusal form a cascading chain that the system cannot break.
surface_narrative: Students face hunger, trauma, addiction at home, and school refusal — and schools are expected to solve everything alone.
implicit_narrative: The education system has become a de facto mental health and social welfare provider without the resources or mandate.
metanarrative: Structural violence through neglect: poverty is the root cause, but the system only intervenes at the symptom level (school refusal, behaviour). The causal chain demands upstream investment, not downstream crisis management.
representative_quotes: I believe the main challenge is poverty and its knock on effects to our students. We have students who are hungry and unhygienic.
The trauma of growing up in this area impacts the majority of our students in many different ways.

name: Traveller MH & stigma
key_idea: Cultural barriers, shame, and intergenerational trauma block access to mental health support.
surface_narrative: Traveller communities face cultural stigma around mental health, making help-seeking extremely difficult.
implicit_narrative: Shame and community silence are more powerful than any service offer. The system does not understand Traveller cultural context.
metanarrative: Cultural safety as a precondition for care: without trust, cultural humility, and community-embedded services, mental health provision is structurally inaccessible to marginalised groups.
representative_quotes: There is a huge stigma in the Traveller community around mental health.

name: Sports / inclusive activity
key_idea: Activity-based interventions build confidence, social capital, and belonging.
surface_narrative: Sports and inclusive activities improve mental health, confidence, and social connection, especially for marginalised youth.
implicit_narrative: The real value is not physical activity but the relational infrastructure — trusted adults, peer belonging, safe spaces.
metanarrative: The social prescription frame: health is produced in communities, not clinics. Investment in social infrastructure (youth clubs, sports, arts) is health investment by another name.
representative_quotes: I think my favourite of all the things so far has been the teen hub because I have made some great friends there.
I am still quite an anxious person, but I am not nearly as bad as I used to be.
